---
title: "Chat and Branching"
---

# Chat and Branching

At the core of Mayon is the branchable chat graph. Every conversation is a node in a tree. When an AI response covers too much ground or introduces a thread you want to follow deeper, you can branch a new chat from any highlighted span.

## Starting a chat

Open the chat view and type a prompt in the composer at the bottom of the screen. The AI streams a response in real time. Responses render as markdown with syntax highlighting for code blocks.

Each chat is assigned a provider and model at creation time. You can change the active provider and model from the chat view before sending your first message.

## Highlighting and branching

When the AI finishes a response, click and drag to select a span of text within any assistant message. A floating action appears over the selection with a "Branch from here" option.

Clicking it creates a new child chat that:

- Inherits the full conversation history up to the fork point (every message from the parent and all its ancestors, in order).
- Injects the highlighted excerpt as a branching seed so the child's first turn is anchored to that specific text.
- Appears as a child node in the sidebar tree.

The parent's context is **referenced**, not copied. The child reads ancestor messages up to the branch point each time it assembles context for the LLM. This means storage is lightweight — a branch does not duplicate its parent's messages.

## Tree navigation

The sidebar shows the conversation tree. Each root chat is a top-level node. Branches appear as indented children. Click any node to jump to that chat.

The tree supports:

- **Expand/collapse** — click the chevron to show or hide children.
- **Depth display** — the tree renders the depth of each node for visual context.
- **Active indicator** — the currently viewed chat is highlighted.

## Breadcrumbs

Above the chat view, a breadcrumb trail shows the path from the current chat back to the root ancestor. Each breadcrumb is clickable — tap any ancestor in the path to jump to it.

## Cross-links

You can link any two chats together with a cross-link, even if they belong to different trees. Cross-links are references between otherwise separate conversations, annotated with an optional note explaining the connection.

Cross-linked chats appear as distinct reference edges in the sidebar (not as parent-child relationships). This lets you build a web of related conversations without flattening them into a single tree.

## Context assembly

When you send a message in a branched chat, Mayon assembles the LLM context by walking up the tree:

1. Collect all messages in the current chat.
2. For each ancestor, collect its messages up to the branch point recorded by the child that links into it.
3. Sort all collected messages by depth (ascending) and then by order within each chat.
4. Inject the original branch excerpt as a system note anchoring the conversation.

Messages created in a parent **after** a fork are never included in the child's context. This isolation is what makes branching useful — each branch explores a direction without interference from later turns in its ancestors.
